title_en: Author Biography and His Universal Islamic History
title_id: Biografi Pengarang dan Sejarah Islam Universalnya
content_en:
Imad ad-Din Abu al-Fida Ismail ibn Umar ibn Kathir al-Qurashi al-Busrawi was born in Busra in the Hauran region of Syria around 701 AH (1301 CE). He moved to Damascus as a child after the death of his father, where he studied under the major scholars of the city — most significantly under the Hanbali reformer Ibn Taymiyyah and under the hadith scholar al-Mizzi, whose daughter he eventually married.

Ibn Kathir became a Shafi'i scholar of the highest rank, teaching at the Great Mosque of Damascus (the Umayyad Mosque) and at several madrasas in the city. He was known for his mastery of tafsir, hadith, and history, and his scholarly output across these fields was remarkable. His relationship with Ibn Taymiyyah, while intellectually formative, also exposed him to the political controversies surrounding his teacher — he was occasionally implicated in the conflicts that Ibn Taymiyyah's positions generated.

His major historical work, Al-Bidayah wal-Nihayah (The Beginning and the End), is a universal Islamic history from the creation of the world to the year 768 AH (1366 CE), near the end of Ibn Kathir's own life. Running to approximately fourteen to twenty-two volumes depending on the edition, it is one of the most comprehensive works of Islamic historical writing and remains a standard reference.

Al-Bidayah wal-Nihayah begins with a Quranic-based account of creation, moves through the histories of the pre-Islamic prophets and peoples, covers the life of the Prophet Muhammad in substantial detail, and then proceeds through the political history of the Islamic world from the Rashidun caliphate through the Mamluk period. Its combination of historical narrative with hadith evaluation and theological concern gives it a character distinct from purely annalistic works.

Ibn Kathir died in Damascus in 774 AH (1373 CE), leaving behind this historical monument alongside his celebrated Tafsir al-Quran al-Azim.
content_id:
Imad al-Din Abu al-Fida' Ismail ibn Umar ibn Katsir al-Qurasyi al-Bushrawi lahir di Bushra di kawasan Hauran Suriah sekitar 701 H (1301 M). Beliau pindah ke Damaskus sebagai anak kecil setelah wafatnya ayahnya, di mana beliau belajar kepada ulama-ulama utama kota itu — paling signifikan kepada reformis Hanbali Ibn Taimiyyah dan kepada ulama hadis al-Mizzi, yang putrinya akhirnya dinikahi oleh Ibn Katsir.

Ibn Katsir menjadi ulama Syafi'i dengan pangkat tertinggi, mengajar di Masjid Agung Damaskus (Masjid Umayyah) dan di beberapa madrasah di kota tersebut. Beliau dikenal karena penguasaannya atas tafsir, hadis, dan sejarah, dan hasil karya ilmiahnya di bidang-bidang ini sangat luar biasa. Hubungannya dengan Ibn Taimiyyah, meskipun secara intelektual sangat membentuk, juga mengeksposnya pada kontroversi-kontroversi politik yang mengelilingi gurunya — beliau kadang-kadang terlibat dalam konflik-konflik yang ditimbulkan oleh posisi-posisi Ibn Taimiyyah.

Karya historis utamanya, Al-Bidayah wal-Nihayah (Permulaan dan Pengakhiran), adalah sejarah Islam universal dari penciptaan dunia hingga tahun 768 H (1366 M), menjelang akhir kehidupan Ibn Katsir sendiri. Mencakup sekitar empat belas hingga dua puluh dua jilid tergantung edisinya, ini adalah salah satu karya penulisan sejarah Islam yang paling komprehensif dan tetap menjadi referensi standar.

Al-Bidayah wal-Nihayah dimulai dengan kisah penciptaan berbasis al-Quran, bergerak melalui sejarah-sejarah para nabi dan umat pra-Islam, mencakup kehidupan Nabi Muhammad ﷺ secara cukup terperinci, kemudian melanjutkan melalui sejarah politik dunia Islam dari kekhalifahan Rasyidin hingga periode Mamluk. Kombinasinya antara narasi historis dengan evaluasi hadis dan kepedulian teologis memberinya karakter yang berbeda dari karya-karya analistik semata.

Ibn Katsir wafat di Damaskus pada 774 H (1373 M), meninggalkan monumen historis ini bersama Tafsir al-Quran al-'Adzim-nya yang termasyhur.
